# servicenow_ui_page (Resource)

`servicenow_ui_page` manages a UI Page configuration within ServiceNow.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Manages a UI Page (HTML + client/server scripts) in ServiceNow.
resource "servicenow_ui_page" "example" {
  name              = "example_page"
  category          = "general"
  html              = "<h1>Example Page</h1>"
  client_script     = "// client-side script"
  processing_script = "// server-side processing script"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `client_script` (String) Client-side JavaScript to be executed on the UI Page.
- `html` (String) The HTML body of the UI Page.
- `name` (String) Name of the UI Page.
- `processing_script` (String) Server-side script to be executed when the UI Page loads.

### Optional

- `category` (String) Category the UI Page belongs to.
- `description` (String) Description of the UI Page.
- `direct` (Boolean) If set to 'true', this UI Page can be accessed directly via URL without being embedded in the ServiceNow frame.
- `protection_policy` (String) Determines how application files are protected when downloaded or installed. Can be empty for no protection, 'read' for read-only protection or 'protected'.
- `scope` (String) Associates a resource to a specific application ID in ServiceNow.

### Read-Only

- `endpoint` (String) The URL endpoint for accessing this UI Page.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using the sys_id of the record in ServiceNow.
terraform import servicenow_ui_page.example <sys_id>
```
